name: Airtable MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server that gives AI systems full read/write access to Airtable bases, including record CRUD, schema management, field creation, and record comments.
use_cases: Query and update Airtable records via natural language through an AI assistant
Automate Airtable schema management (create tables, add fields)
Search across Airtable records with full-text search
Add comments to Airtable records programmatically
Build AI-powered workflows on top of Airtable data
not_for: Replacing Airtable's native automations for high-volume batch operations
best_when: You use Airtable as a data backend and want an AI assistant to read, write, and manage your bases through natural language.
avoid_when: You need high-throughput batch operations, real-time sync, or are working with databases other than Airtable.

auth.notes: Requires Airtable Personal Access Token (PAT) via AIRTABLE_API_KEY env var. Tokens are scoped per-base with granular permissions: schema.bases:read, data.records:read, schema.bases:write, data.records:write, data.recordComments:read/write.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Subject to Airtable's own API rate limits (5 requests/sec per base).
pricing.notes: MIT licensed. The MCP server is free. Airtable itself has free and paid tiers. API access is available on all Airtable plans.

performance.rate_limits: Airtable API: 5 requests per second per base

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No explicit documentation on error message formats, but TypeScript implementation likely returns structured errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Record updates use Airtable record IDs which are stable, but no explicit idempotency keys documented.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP transport mode has NO built-in authentication - must be behind a reverse proxy
Token scopes must be configured per-base, easy to miss required permissions
Airtable rate limit of 5 req/sec per base can throttle busy agents
delete_records is destructive with no undo - agents should confirm before deletion

security_score_components.security_notes: Uses Airtable Personal Access Token. Same security profile as Airtable API — scoped to specific bases/tables. Community-maintained server — review before production use. Rate limit of 5 req/sec still applies through MCP.
